description:
A developer has handed you the working directory of a small‐scale distributed
    system project located in “/home/user/project”.  
    Your job is two-fold:  
        1.  Re–organise the project tree into a clean structure.  
        2.  Produce a concise, machine-readable error report from the node logs
            so that QA can quickly spot stability problems.

    STEP-BY-STEP REQUIREMENTS
    -------------------------
    1.  Directory clean-up  
        a.  Inside “/home/user/project” create the sub-directories exactly named:
                • src
                • config
                • scripts
        b.  Move files according to their extension **without** changing their
            filenames:  
                • “*.py”        →  src  
                • “*.conf”      →  config  
                • “*.sh”        →  scripts  
            The original top-level copies must no longer exist once the move is
            finished.

    2.  Manifest generation  
        a.  Create a new file “/home/user/project/manifest.csv”.  
        b.  The file must be **comma-separated with NO spaces** and contain **one
            line per file that was moved in step 1**.  
        c.  Columns (in this order):  
                1) relative_path_from_/home/user/project  
                2) lowercase SHA-256 digest of the file’s new content  
        d.  Example format (do **NOT** include this example line):  
                src/app.py,9f2c…e41b

    3.  Log analysis  
        a.  A folder “/home/user/project/logs” already contains three log files.
            Each line is semicolon-delimited in the form:  
                YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ;LEVEL;NODE_ID;MESSAGE  
        b.  Parse every line of every “*.log” file found directly under
            “…/logs”.  
        c.  Count, per NODE_ID, the number of lines whose LEVEL equals exactly
            “ERROR” and the number of lines whose LEVEL equals exactly “WARN”.  
        d.  Create the summary file
            “/home/user/project/logs/error_summary.log” **with the exact
            structure shown below** so that automated tests can compare it:  

                # Distributed System Error Summary
                Generated:<ISO-8601-timestamp>
                NodeID,ERRORS,WARNINGS
                node-id-1,<error-count-1>,<warn-count-1>
                node-id-2,<error-count-2>,<warn-count-2>
                …

            Formatting rules the checker will enforce:  
                • The very first line must start with “# Distributed System Error
                  Summary” (no leading spaces).  
                • The second line must start with “Generated:” followed
                  immediately by an ISO-8601 timestamp (the exact value is not
                  tested, only pattern).  
                • The third line is the header shown above.  
                • From the fourth line onward, list one node per line, **sorted
                  by the ASCII value of NodeID (ascending)**.  
                • NodeID is case-sensitive and must appear exactly as found in
                  the log lines.  
                • Separate the three columns with commas and **no extra spaces**.

    4.  Verification artefact  
        a.  Create “/home/user/project/organize_report.txt”.  
        b.  The file contains exactly three lines in this exact order:  
                MOVED_FILES:<number_of_files_moved_in_step_1>  
                MANIFEST_LINES:<number_of_data_lines_in_manifest.csv>  
                SUMMARY_NODES:<number_of_unique_NodeIDs_processed>  

    5.  Do **not** delete or rename any other files or directories that already
        existed.

    SUCCESS CRITERIA
    ----------------
    • Directories “src”, “config” and “scripts” exist and contain the correct
      files while those files are no longer present at the project root.  
    • “/home/user/project/manifest.csv” exists, has as many data lines as files
      moved and every digest is a valid 64-character hexadecimal string.  
    • “/home/user/project/logs/error_summary.log” follows the required template
      exactly and has the correct per-node counts.  
    • “/home/user/project/organize_report.txt” exists, contains exactly three
      colon-separated lines with the correct integers, and ends with a newline.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent runs):

    /home/user/project
    ├── app.py
    ├── helper.py
    ├── default.conf
    ├── run.sh
    ├── logs
    │   ├── cluster_node_A.log
    │   ├── cluster_node_B.log
    │   └── cluster_node_C.log
    └── (no other files/dirs)

    Contents of the three pre-supplied log files:

    1) /home/user/project/logs/cluster_node_A.log
        2023-07-15T12:01:03Z;INFO;NodeA;Heartbeat OK
        2023-07-15T12:01:10Z;ERROR;NodeA;Timeout contacting service registry
        2023-07-15T12:02:11Z;WARN;NodeA;Slow response from cache
        2023-07-15T12:03:45Z;ERROR;NodeA;Unhandled exception in worker thread

    2) /home/user/project/logs/cluster_node_B.log
        2023-07-15T12:00:02Z;WARN;NodeB;Clock skew detected
        2023-07-15T12:01:59Z;ERROR;NodeB;Failed to replicate log entry
        2023-07-15T12:04:00Z;WARN;NodeB;Retrying connection to NodeC

    3) /home/user/project/logs/cluster_node_C.log
        2023-07-15T12:00:30Z;INFO;NodeC;Startup complete
        2023-07-15T12:02:47Z;WARN;NodeC;Low disk space

    Expected counts the grading scripts will look for:

        NodeA  ->  ERROR=2 , WARN=1
        NodeB  ->  ERROR=1 , WARN=2
        NodeC  ->  ERROR=0 , WARN=1

    Therefore, after the agent finishes,
    /home/user/project/logs/error_summary.log must contain exactly:

        # Distributed System Error Summary
        Generated:<any ISO-8601 datetime>
        NodeID,ERRORS,WARNINGS
        NodeA,2,1
        NodeB,1,2
        NodeC,0,1

    (The “Generated:” timestamp may differ.)

    Files that MUST be listed inside manifest.csv (and therefore moved):

        src/app.py
        src/helper.py
        config/default.conf
        scripts/run.sh

    As a consequence:
        • manifest.csv will contain 4 data lines.
        • organize_report.txt will show:
            MOVED_FILES:4
            MANIFEST_LINES:4
            SUMMARY_NODES:3

    The autograder will verify:
        • Existence of the tree “src”, “config”, “scripts”.
        • Absence of the moved files in /home/user/project.
        • SHA-256 digests in manifest.csv match the post-move file contents.
        • The summary file structure and counts match the values above.
        • organize_report.txt contains the three exact colon-separated lines
          with the integer values 4, 4 and 3.
